M.label: Monthly
M.note: FADA remains the anchor source for the latest PV OEM retail snapshot. YoY, current market share and share change come from the validated FADA annexure. Additional monthly-window fields stay as n.m. until broader structured OEM history is wired across FADA, SIAM, Rushlane and Parivahan.

Q.label: Quarterly
Q.note: The quarterly PV view keeps the current validated FADA OEM retail ranking in place and exposes the requested quarter-window schema. Quarter-window growth fields will populate as we add structured OEM history from the public source chain.

Y.label: Yearly
Y.note: The yearly PV view keeps the latest validated FADA OEM retail table as the anchor while reserving long-horizon growth columns for structured OEM history. This avoids inventing annual growth where the current snapshot does not yet carry a validated multi-year OEM series.

bullets: Mar 2026 retail closed at 2,692,449 units with +25.28% YoY growth and +11.75% MoM momentum.
Two-wheelers led volumes at 1,951,006 units (+28.68% YoY), while passenger vehicles hit 440,144 units (+21.48% YoY).
PV inventory stayed healthy at about 28 days, keeping wholesale-retail alignment much tighter than a year ago.
Dealer outlook stayed constructive but measured, with positive demand expectations for both the next month and the next three months.

components.title: Components

source_meta.name: ACMA
source_meta.note: ACMA notes that tyres, batteries, paints and consumables are excluded from industry scope; EV component sales to domestic OEMs are included under OEM supply figures, excluding batteries.

components.headline: Indian auto component industry clocks turnover of Rs 6.73 lakh crore in FY25.
insights: Domestic OEM demand remained the largest growth driver, supported by about 8% growth in vehicle production.
Exports stayed healthy despite geopolitical disruptions, with North America still the largest market and Asia the fastest-growing region.
Aftermarket demand stayed firm on a larger used-vehicle base, formalisation of repairs, and rising e-commerce penetration.
ACMA specifically flags rare-earth magnet availability as a strategic watchpoint for EV and mobility manufacturing.

listed_beneficiaries.summary: Key listed proxy for PV, CV and electric passenger vehicles.
Diversified exposure across UVs, LCVs, 3W last-mile mobility and tractors.
Broad 2W exposure plus 3W participation and e-2W optionality.
Strong listed proxy for both motorcycles and 3W passenger/goods demand.
Listed 3W niche exposure with potential benefit from last-mile demand.
Battery beneficiary from EV penetration and replacement demand.
Battery and energy-storage proxy with EV transition leverage.
EV drivetrain content proxy tied to electrification mix gains.
CV cycle plus component export and EV platform optionality.
Broad content supplier across lighting, switches and new mobility programs.
Large diversified component export proxy with OEM cycle sensitivity.
Bus and electric mobility exposure with public transport optionality.
Electric bus proxy rather than broad auto demand beta.
Small but relevant e-mobility and last-mile ecosystem proxy.

source_meta.name: World Bank Pink Sheet
source_meta.note: Annual averages for 2022 to 2025 plus the latest visible February 2026 quote. Iron ore is shown as a steel-input proxy for auto materials.

insights.title: Segment speed
insights.body: Two-Wheelers were the fastest-growing retail category in Mar 2026 at +28.68% YoY, while Construction Equipment stayed weakest at -16.17% YoY.

insights.title: OEM share shifts
insights.body: In PV retail, Maruti Suzuki gained the most share at +2.16 pp YoY, while Hyundai Motor India lost -1.06 pp. In 2W, the biggest gainer was TVS Motor at +0.98 pp, while Ola Electric was the sharpest loser at -1.04 pp.

insights.title: Retail vs wholesale
insights.body: PV retail ran at 105.4% of wholesale in February 2026, versus 104.3% for 2W. The tighter PV ratio sits alongside FADA's 27-29 day inventory signal, suggesting channel conditions have improved materially.

insights.title: EV transition
insights.body: Derived EV penetration in February 2026 was 10.38% of retail volume. The strongest EV mix remains in Three-Wheelers at 57.89%, while PV EV share stayed below 4%, which keeps battery, drivetrain and power-electronics suppliers more leveraged than OEM headline units alone.

insights.title: Component read-across
insights.body: ACMA's FY25 review still points to a healthy ancillary backdrop: OEM supplies grew 10% YoY, exports grew 8%, and aftermarket grew 6%. That supports continued interest in export-capable, value-added suppliers even if near-term OEM mix shifts month to month.

company_map.summary: Management said FY25 growth remained led by the combined cranes, material handling and construction equipment segment, which grew meaningfully faster than the agriculture equipment business. The annual report also stressed a more agile operating model and faster alignment to customer demand, reinforcing ACE's core exposure to infrastructure and industrial equipment cycles.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said growth is being driven by robust OEM demand in both 4W and 2W, healthy aftermarket volumes and rising realisation from exports, while the new-energy business is gaining traction in telecom and stationary applications. It also said the customer qualification plant for indigenous cell manufacturing should materially accelerate the company's lithium-ion roadmap.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said the FY25 CV backdrop stayed supported by infrastructure activity, stronger rural demand, robust bus replacement and a recovery in export markets. It highlighted record profitability, highest-ever MHCV bus volumes, improving exports and fresh electric-truck deliveries as evidence that growth is broadening beyond a single sub-segment.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said FY25 delivered robust growth in three-wheeler volumes, revenue and profitability, helped by stronger market penetration and better operating efficiency. It also highlighted improving exports and wider market expansion as signs that the company is participating in both domestic last-mile demand and overseas three-wheeler opportunities.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management described Q3 FY26 as a record quarter, driven by double-digit growth across domestic motorcycles, electric two-wheelers, three-wheelers and exports. It also highlighted that the electric portfolio reached about a quarter of domestic revenues, while Chetak and e-3W momentum improved sharply after supply bottlenecks eased.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Bharat Forge is positioning less for a conventional cycle and more for structural shifts across automotive, defence, aerospace and industrials. The FY25 annual report highlighted growing traction in domestic passenger vehicles, interest from global customers under China-plus-one, and a large defence order book as the key read-through for future growth.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Royal Enfield crossed one million annual sales with exports growing faster than domestic volumes, while overseas assembly and local-market expansion are improving long-term reach. On the CV side, VECV beat industry growth, delivered record volumes and strengthened its light and medium-duty truck position despite a mixed market backdrop.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management framed FY25 around sustained leadership in tractors while using construction equipment and other non-tractor businesses to diversify the revenue base. The annual report also pointed to dealer expansion, financing support in emerging markets and the Kubota partnership as key levers to strengthen premium utility tractors and long-term farm mechanisation demand.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Q3 FY26 demand recovered strongly across auto OEM, replacement, inverter and industrial segments, with GST 2.0 helping automotive demand. The company also pointed to continued investment behind lithium-ion cells and a preference for profitable growth while preserving balance-sheet flexibility.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Greaves is building a fuel-agnostic, digitally integrated mobility ecosystem rather than relying on a single propulsion trend. The annual report and recent results commentary both point to growth in engineering and retail, deeper reach in tier-3 and tier-4 markets, and continued investment in EV solutions, components and last-mile applications.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Q3 FY26 delivered healthy double-digit volume and retail momentum, helped by improving rural demand and a supportive GST tailwind. The company said its focus remains on mix improvement, scooters, premium products, VIDA electric mobility and broader international expansion.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Hyundai is leaning further into SUV-led premiumisation while widening its ICE, CNG and EV portfolio. The FY25 annual report also framed Talegaon, Chennai EV capacity, and 26 model upgrades and launches through FY30 as the next leg of volume and mix growth.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said JBM is not just selling buses but building an end-to-end electric mobility ecosystem across buses, batteries, charging, software and mobility services. The FY25 annual report also highlighted growing scale in e-buses, strong auto-components execution and in-house EV aggregates as the core pillars of the next growth phase.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Auto and Farm maintained leadership on the back of steady customer demand, strong product acceptance and disciplined execution. On the Q3 FY26 callout, it highlighted better SUV and LCV share, a still-dominant tractor franchise and a very positive early response to new launches.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Q3 FY26 saw a sharp recovery in the Indian car market, led mainly by small cars after the GST reform. The company highlighted record domestic sales, record total sales and continued export momentum, signalling confidence that demand breadth has improved rather than being limited to premium segments.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Olectra's electric-bus strategy is increasingly about fleet reliability, route relevance and technology upgrades rather than just unit sales. The FY25 annual report pointed to over 2,600 deployed buses, strong monthly operating mileage, work on blade-battery buses and roughly a quarter of India's e-bus market share as proof of execution scale.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said demand tailwinds continue to come from rising vehicle content, premiumisation, SUVs and the accelerating hybrid and EV mix across major regions. The annual report also emphasised disciplined cost control, customer engagement and supply-chain agility as the way Motherson plans to convert that structural content growth into resilient earnings.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said Sona Comstar is moving from a traditional component base toward intelligent mobility systems, with BEV revenue rising sharply and EV programmes continuing to scale. The FY25 annual report also underlined bigger bets in China, deeper R&D, and a broader mobility-solutions portfolio as the next growth drivers.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said growth in Q3 FY26 was broad-based across motorcycles, scooters, exports, three-wheelers and EVs, with each of those legs contributing to record sales, revenue and profit. The read-through is that the company still sees room to compound through both domestic premiumisation and international momentum.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: In the latest official CV-side Q3 FY26 update after the business restructuring, management said healthy demand across key segments and tighter execution are translating into profitable growth, double-digit margins and strong free cash flow. The company also said it remains confident of delivering on its stated financial guidance.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

company_map.summary: Management said the Indian auto industry still has a strong growth runway and Uno Minda is positioning for that through localisation, export expansion and rising content in LED lighting, ADAS, EV systems and alternate-fuel products. It also flagged new wins in EV chargers, camera modules and CNG components as proof that the content story is widening beyond traditional auto cycles.

revenue_table.note: Revenue rows are aligned to official reportable segments disclosed in annual reports. Where a company reports as a single operating segment, the table shows that disclosed business line at 100% share.

items.title: Porsche loses speed on a curvy road
items.summary: Global deliveries slumped 15 per cent in the period, dragged down by a 21 per cent decline in China, Porsche said on Friday. European luxury brands are grappling with fierce local competition and an erosion in pricing power in the worlds' biggest car market.

items.title: Volkswagen's first-quarter deliveries drop 4% on weak China, US
items.summary: Once a key growth engine for German carmakers, China has become a major ‌challenge for Volkswagen and rivals Mercedes-Benz and BMW, as a cut-throat price war with fast-moving local brands ​squeezes margins.

items.title: Audi Q1 deliveries fall 6.1% on weak demand in China and North America
items.summary: Deliveries in China, Audi's largest ​market, dropped 12 per cent to 127,109 units, hurt by ​a global slowdown in the automotive sector.

items.title: Nissan Motor India opens 54 new touchpoints in Q1 2026, targets 400 outlets by FY27-end
items.summary: The company said the network push supports its product offensive strategy as it prepares to launch the new Nissan Gravite and upcoming Tekton and C-SUV 7-seater models.

items.title: Porsche deliveries tumble further in first quarter
items.summary: In China, once a major growth engine for ​the Stuttgart-based carmaker, deliveries fell by 21 per cent amid stiff competition on pricing and technology from local ​brands.

items.title: Industry Consortium LEAF Launched to Advance EV Charging Ecosystem for Two- and Three-Wheelers
items.summary: A new industry consortium, the Light Electric-Vehicle Acceleration Forum (LEAF), has been launched to support the development of a more integrated and interoperable charging ecosystem for electric two- and three-wheelers in India. The forum was inaugurated by H.

items.title: Porsche loses speed on a curvy road
items.summary: Porsche's US sales also fell in the first quarter, which it attributed to a high base from stronger Macan EV volumes during last year's launch period and the end of EV buying incentives.

items.title: New Hyundai Venus EV Spied For The First Time
items.summary: Exterior profile of Hyundai Venus test mule appears largely the same as the recently unveiled concept model The post New Hyundai Venus EV Spied For The First Time appeared first on RushLane .

items.title: Stride Green Capital | Scaling India’s commercial EV ecosystem through financing
items.summary: The post Stride Green Capital | Scaling India’s commercial EV ecosystem through financing appeared first at EVreporter on EVreporter . Founded in 2024, Gurugram-headquartered Stride Green Capital Private Limited provides structured asset-leasing and financing solutions in the cleantech sector.

items.title: Stellantis Patents Leapmotor B10 Electric SUV In India – Launch Soon?
items.summary: Leapmotor has patented its upcoming B10 electric SUV in India, indicating that the brand could be moving a step closer to entering the Indian market The post Stellantis Patents Leapmotor B10 Electric SUV In India – Launch Soon? appeared first on RushLane .

items.title: Chery QQ3 EV Patented In India – JSW’s Nexon EV Rival, China Price CNY 59k (Rs 8 L)
items.summary: Chery QQ3 EV has been patented in India, sparking speculations about a possible launch to rival the likes of Nexon EV and Punch EV The post Chery QQ3 EV Patented In India – JSW’s Nexon EV Rival, China Price CNY 59k (Rs 8 L) appeared first on RushLane .

items.title: UK awards £380 million grant to Tata Group’s Agratas for Somerset battery gigafactory
items.summary: The funding will support Agratas’ planned 40 GWh gigafactory in Somerset, expected to become the country’s largest EV battery manufacturing facility and a key supplier to Jaguar Land Rover.

items.title: EV buyers remain TCO conscious despite oil volatility: Santosh Iyer
items.summary: While global fuel disruptions are driving EV curiosity, Mercedes-Benz India says buyers remain pragmatic, evaluating total ownership costs, charging convenience and long-term value before transitioning.

items.title: Sona Comstar Hits 500 Million Differential Gears and 10 Million Differential Assembly Marks
items.summary: Sona BLW Precision Forgings Ltd. (Sona Comstar) announced on April 7, 2026, that it has manufactured 500 million differential gears and 10 million differential assemblies since its founding in 1995, crossing two production milestones that the company says reflect growing customer demand for its driveline components across electric and conventional vehicle platforms globally.

items.title: Valeo commissions e-axle line in Pune for Mahindra’s Born Electric platform
items.summary: French Auto component major Valeo has commissioned a new electric powertrain manufacturing line at its Pune facility, marking a fresh localisation push as it begins supplying integrated e-axles for Mahindra & Mahindra’s upcoming Born Electric vehicles. The newly inaugurated line will produce Valeo’s 3-in-1 e-axle, which integrates the motor, inverter and transmission into a compact unit.

items.title: Motherson Sumi Wiring Faces Near-Term Margin Heat as Copper Prices Climb
items.summary: Motherson Sumi Wiring India Ltd., a dominant player in domestic wiring harness market, is navigating a short-term margin squeeze as it waits for commodity costs to reflect in its contracts. The company, which controls over 55% of the passenger vehicle wiring market and 25% plus in two-wheelers, is currently grappling with a "timing gap" between rising copper prices and customer reimbursements.

items.title: New scheme in works to support domestic critical mineral processing plants: Mines Secretary
items.summary: The Ministry of Mines is planning a new scheme to boost domestic processing of critical minerals and strengthen the value chain, alongside efforts to scale up copper production.

items.title: JSW Group, NLC India among 25 companies in pre-bid meet for ₹7,280 cr rare earth magnet scheme: Sources
items.summary: The ₹7,280-crore initiative aims to select up to five entities to establish facilities with a combined capacity of 6,000 MTPA, crucial for EVs and renewable energy. Bids are due May 28.
